name: Elementary Differential Weight Formula
statement_text:
The value of (310i) is

statement_latex:
\begin{lemma}
The value of \eqref{eq:310i} is
\[
\sum_{t \in T} \frac{h^{\rho(t)}}{\sigma(t)} \theta(t) F(t)(y_0),
\]
where $\theta$ is defined by
\[
\theta(t) = 
\begin{cases}
1, & t = \tau, \\[4pt]
\displaystyle \prod_{i=1}^{k} \theta(t_i), & t = [t_1 t_2 \cdots t_k].
\end{cases}
\]
\end{lemma}
proof_text:
Proof. Use Theorem 306A. The case t = τ is obvious. For t = [tm 1 m2       j


where t1 , t2 , . . . , tj are distinct, the factor
proof_latex:
\begin{proof}
Use Theorem~\ref{thm:306A}. The case $t = \tau$ is obvious. For $t = [t_1^{m_1} t_2^{m_2} \cdots t_j^{m_j}]$, where $t_1, t_2, \dots, t_j$ are distinct, the factor
\[
\sigma(I) \left( \prod_{i=1}^{j} \sigma(t_i)^{m_i} \right)^{-1},
\]
where $I$ is the index set consisting of $m_1$ copies of $1$, $m_2$ copies of $2$, \dots, and $m_j$ copies of $j$, is equal to $\sigma(t)^{-1}$.
\end{proof}

variables.name: F
variables.definition: F(t) is the elementary differential associated with the tree t

equations.content: the formal series expression being evaluated

preamble: The lemma evaluates a formal series (310i) expressed in terms of rooted trees. The notation involves elementary differentials F(t) and tree functions like order r(t) and symmetry σ(t). The function θ is defined recursively on tree structures.
